confidence.disclosure: Reconstructed from Yaqut's own autobiographical remarks in Mu'jam al-Buldan and Mu'jam al-Udaba, supplemented by the surviving 1220 letter he sent from Mosul describing his flight from Khwarazm. The general sequence is well attested but exact years for some early commercial trips for his master Askar are approximate.

sources: Wadie Jwaideh (trans.), The Introductory Chapters of Yaqut's Mu'jam al-Buldan (1959)
D.S. Margoliouth (ed.), Yaqut's Dictionary of Learned Men (Mu'jam al-Udaba, 1907-1927)

journey.event: Born to Greek parents in Byzantine Constantinople; the nisba al-Rumi reflects this origin.
Captured as a child of about five or six and brought as a slave to Baghdad, where he was bought by the merchant Askar ibn Abi Nasr al-Hamawi.
Began travelling on commercial missions for his master, including voyages to the Persian Gulf island of Kish.
Travelled to Oman as Askar's commercial agent, an experience he later recalled as miserable.
Returned to Baghdad to work as an independent copyist and bookseller after his master freed him.
Travelled to Tabriz on a copying and book-selling circuit through Azerbaijan.
Took refuge in Damascus but was forced to flee after a public theological dispute nearly cost him his life.
Sheltered briefly in Aleppo with the patronage of the vizier Ibn al-Qifti.
Passed through Mosul and Irbil on his way east to Khurasan.
Spent two years in the great libraries of Merv, the foundation of his later geographical work, just before the Mongol destruction of the city.
Visited Balkh during his Khurasan circuit.
Recorded Nishapur, which would soon be devastated by the Mongols.
Settled briefly in Khwarazm before the Mongol invasion forced him to flee.
Reached Mosul as a destitute refugee, having lost all his books and possessions to the Mongol advance; wrote his famous letter from here.
Spent some time in Sinjar after the hardships of his Mongol-flight.
Settled in Aleppo under the patronage of Ibn al-Qifti and began composing the first draft of Mu'jam al-Buldan.
Completed the first draft of Mu'jam al-Buldan in Mosul.
Travelled to Alexandria in 1227, his last documented journey.
Returned to Aleppo and completed the final version of Mu'jam al-Buldan.
Died in Aleppo, leaving the Mu'jam al-Buldan as his lasting monument.
